seoTitle: Camp Hill vs Camp Mountain — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Camp Hill and Camp Mountain. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Camp Hill vs Camp Mountain: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Camp Hill and Camp Mountain.
heading: Camp Hill vs Camp Mountain — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Camp Hill, Queensland and Camp Mountain, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Camp Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Camp Mountain (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Camp Hill or Camp Mountain safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Camp Hill has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Camp Mountain scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Camp Hill scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Camp Mountain scores 39/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Camp Hill or Camp Mountain?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Camp Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
